mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate labor income, which feeds into consumption and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises the risk-free discount rate and tightens financial conditions, exerting downward pressure on equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated demand-supply contraction that reduces corporate revenue visibility and triggers de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns that are larger than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change negative and INDPRO 3-month change negative
failure_condition: Joint negative signal produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated, acceleration in industrial production signals capacity constraints and margin-eroding cost pressures, flipping the typical growth-equity relationship so that faster IP growth predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive in >50% of observations when INDPRO accelerates under elevated CPIAUCSL, or the sign of the relationship is not reversed versus low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Momentum in housing starts captures near-term construction activity and durable goods demand that translates into immediate earnings sentiment shifts, driving short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST 1-month change (Spearman rho < 0.03), or positive return frequency < 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive policy regimes, month-over-month declines in consumer prices signal successful disinflation that reduces the required real return premium and extends the equity earnings multiple, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in >50% of triggered disinflation instances under restrictive policy, or mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month jump in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession reassessment and near-term earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in >55% of instances following a 0.2pp UNRATE jump, or conditional mean is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP confirms the persistence of aggregate demand expansion, supporting baseline corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
